justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified as domain model change — business_context calls out 'tight coupling where loose coupling previously existed by design' and rejection_reasons flag bounded context violation. (2) Data ownership question raised — BR-5 states stock truth must be owned by Warehouse, escalation #3 asks who owns Reservation. (3) TOCTOU/race condition identified — BR-4 explains two concurrent orders seeing same stock, AC-3 tests simultaneous orders, edge case #1 covers race during pick confirmation. (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction surfaced — ubiquitous_language defines Stock Reservation as 'currently MISSING from both contexts', BR-4 establishes reservation as invariant for concurrency correctness. (5) Escalation to MJ for architectural coupling — four detailed escalations covering block semantics, staleness window, reservation ownership, and partial fulfillment. (6) New cross-context invariant stated — BR-2 defines the order-cannot-exceed-stock invariant across contexts.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough context coupling analysis — not only identifies the coupling but explains why it existed by design and what violating it means
TOCTOU problem addressed from multiple angles: business rule (BR-4), acceptance criterion (AC-3), edge case (race condition), and the reservation mechanism as the solution
Stock Reservation correctly identified as a MISSING domain concept prerequisite, not just a nice-to-have — with ownership options analyzed in escalation
Rejection reasons section adds value by flagging where the original spec itself violates domain principles (Sales enforcing stock, 'real time' being architecturally dishonest)
Confidence level of 45 is well-calibrated given the fundamental ambiguities identified — demonstrates intellectual honesty
Edge cases go beyond the rubric requirements — reservation expiry, stock adjustments below reservations, blocked order recovery, and split shipment are all real-world scenarios
Escalations don't explicitly name MJ as the target, though they are clearly architectural escalations that would route to MJ in the team workflow

This crosses two established bounded contexts and introduces tight coupling where loose coupling previously existed by design.","bounded_context":"Cross-context integration between Warehouse Management and Sales/Order Management.
